body:
## Summary
- Add typed validation/loading helpers for `.agent/sepo-version.json`.
- Add a `print-sepo-version` CLI that prints JSON or a compact summary and writes GitHub Actions outputs.
- Document CLI usage and source metadata validation rules.

## Tests
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/sepo-version.test.js`
- `node .agent/dist/cli/print-sepo-version.js --json`
- `(cd .agent && node dist/cli/print-sepo-version.js --json)`
- `git diff --check`
- `npm --prefix .agent run test -- --test-name-pattern sepo`

Closes #132

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | PR is limited to version metadata runtime access: one reader module, one CLI, focused tests, and two docs edits. No unrelated workflow/orchestrator changes. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/technical-details/versioning.md` with CLI usage/output contract and updates install docs to point to the diagnostic CLI. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | CLI exposes separate `--path <metadata-json>` and `--json` flags instead of overloading one argument, and validates malformed/unknown arguments clearly. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses existing `.agent/src/output.ts` `setOutput` helper for GitHub Actions outputs instead of adding another output writer. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | Implementation matches linked issue #132 and parent #74 scope: narrow reader/validator/CLI after PR #130, explicitly avoiding updater, failure-report, and release workflow work. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Invalid metadata returns exit code `1`, writes a clear stderr error, and has regression coverage for malformed metadata instead of producing misleading empty outputs. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Uses a small typed module plus direct CLI wrapper; validation is explicit and local without introducing extra framework or broad architecture. |

## Notes

- The strongest score drivers are scope discipline, focused test coverage, and docs staying aligned with the new user-facing CLI.
- Route/process rubrics about delegated authorization, merge strategy, preview contracts, and orchestration follow-up policy were not scored because this PR does not change those behaviors.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific issues found.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #133 implements the `version-metadata-runtime-access` slice for issue #132, which is part of the broader versioning plan in #74.
- It adds typed validation/loading for `.agent/sepo-version.json`, a `print-sepo-version` CLI, focused tests, and documentation updates.
- The intended scope is narrow: runtime metadata access only, with updater behavior, failure-report integration, and release workflows intentionally deferred.
- Reviewers agreed the PR is well-scoped and generally fits the project’s existing patterns.

## Review
The implementation is solid overall, but one validator issue should be fixed before treating the metadata contract as reliable. I posted one inline comment for the concrete changed-line finding.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `source_repo` validator accepts path-like slugs | WARNING | `SOURCE_REPO_RE` accepts malformed values such as `./repo`, `owner/..`, and invalid owner slugs, despite documenting the field as a GitHub `owner/repo` slug. |
| Non-blocking metadata polish notes | INFO | One reviewer noted optional follow-ups around escaping `[` in a regex, documenting/testing 64-char SHA support, and clarifying null outputs as empty strings. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific follow-up.
- Reviewers agreed the PR correctly avoids out-of-scope updater, failure-report, and release workflow changes.
- Existing PR discussion did not already cover the validator issue; an inline comment was posted on `.agent/src/sepo-version.ts` line 53.
- Reported verification passed for build, focused tests, CLI smoke checks, diff check, and the full `.agent` test suite.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>`source_repo` validator accepts path-like slugs</summary>

**Cause:**  
[`SOURCE_REPO_RE`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/c6dce2799a180ba9eff46a39f7daebd619573302/.agent/src/sepo-version.ts#L53) validates the whole field with a broad character class, so path-like or non-GitHub owner values can pass.

**Candidate solutions:**  
Validate owner and repo segments separately. At minimum, reject `.` / `..` path segments and invalid owner characters, and add focused negative tests for examples that currently pass.

**Comments:**  
Codex treated this as the only substantive issue. Claude’s review was otherwise SHIP-level, with only INFO polish notes.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the remaining required work is a small, concrete validator/test change that is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Tighten `source_repo` validation and add focused negative tests for malformed slugs that currently pass.

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

Address only the latest review synthesis action items:
- Tighten `source_repo` validation and add focused negative tests for malformed slugs that currently pass.

Constraints: Ignore optional INFO notes, metadata-only polish, already-fixed findings, and human-judgment nits unless required by those action items.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777887898858 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUzMTE4NzU4OTA6cmV2aWV3OjEzMzpmaXhfcHI6MTMzOjQ -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-132/codex-25311618715`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

- Tightened `source_repo` validation to split `owner/repo`, require a GitHub-style owner segment, and reject path-like repo segments such as `.` / `..`.
- Added negative coverage for malformed slugs including `./repo`, `owner/..`, and invalid owner slugs.
- Verified with `npm --prefix .agent run build`, `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/sepo-version.test.js`, `npm --prefix .agent run test -- --test-name-pattern sepo`, and `git diff --check`.

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |

| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates [`versioning.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/19acd881c20fb001dcd80a1a371bf3161bb41067/.agent/docs/technical-details/versioning.md) with CLI usage/output contract and updates install docs to reference the diagnostic CLI. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | CLI uses separate `--path <metadata-json>` and `--json` flags, validates missing/unknown args clearly, and writes named GitHub Actions outputs. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | [`print-sepo-version.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/19acd881c20fb001dcd80a1a371bf3161bb41067/.agent/src/cli/print-sepo-version.ts) reuses the existing `setOutput` helper instead of introducing separate Actions-output handling. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR scope matches linked #132 and parent #74: the runtime metadata reader/CLI slice after #130, without reopening the larger #128-style scope. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Invalid metadata exits nonzero with stderr, and tests cover malformed metadata plus stricter `source_repo` rejection cases after the fix-pr pass. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Implementation is a small typed module plus direct CLI wrapper with local validation, no new framework or broad architecture. |

## Notes

- Main score drivers: narrow scope, docs aligned with the new user-facing CLI, clear validation failures, and focused tests.
- Route/process rubrics such as delegated route authorization, one-shot orchestration defaults, merge strategy, preview contracts, and status-comment formatting were not scored because this PR does not change those behaviors.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific issues found.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #133 implements issue #132, the `version-metadata-runtime-access` slice under the broader versioning plan in #74.
- It adds typed loading/validation for `.agent/sepo-version.json`, a `print-sepo-version` CLI, tests, and documentation updates.
- The PR intentionally stays out of updater behavior, failure-report integration, and release automation.
- A prior review found weak `source_repo` validation; the current head includes the follow-up fix and focused regression coverage.

## Review
Current head looks ready to ship. Codex and rubrics review found no actionable defects; Claude raised optional polish notes, but none require a branch change before merge.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No unresolved actionable issues | INFO | The prior validator finding has been fixed, and the remaining reviewer notes are optional polish rather than merge-blocking defects. |

## Progress
- Earlier inline feedback on `.agent/src/sepo-version.ts` covered the `source_repo` validator weakness.
- The follow-up fix-pr pass tightened `source_repo` validation and added negative tests for malformed slugs.
- Latest rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific follow-up.
- Latest Codex review reports build, focused tests, CLI smoke test, diff check, and full `.agent` test suite passed.
- Claude noted optional cleanup around regex escaping, `--path --json` handling, and future-proofing, but still gave a SHIP verdict.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: no unresolved required branch-change work remains, and remaining polish is optional maintainer judgment.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.
@sepo-agent /answer but why do we need this cli? can't we just make it simple (i.e. reading from the json file whatever?

You're right that the **source of truth should stay simple**: `.agent/sepo-version.json`.

The CLI in this PR is not meant to replace that. It is a thin wrapper around the typed reader in [`sepo-version.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/19acd881c20fb001dcd80a1a371bf3161bb41067/.agent/src/sepo-version.ts), mainly so workflows and diagnostics can consume the file without duplicating parsing, validation, and GitHub Actions output handling.

Why keep it:

- Workflows need named outputs like `version`, `source_sha`, `summary`, etc. The CLI writes those using the existing [`setOutput`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/19acd881c20fb001dcd80a1a371bf3161bb41067/.agent/src/output.ts) helper.
- Bug reports and update checks can run one stable command instead of each adding their own `jq` / inline Node parsing.
- Invalid metadata should fail loudly. Raw JSON reads make it easy for future workflows to accidentally accept malformed version/source data.
- The repo already uses this pattern heavily: small `node .agent/dist/cli/*.js` wrappers around typed helpers for workflow-safe behavior.

So the simple model is:

- JSON file: canonical data.
- [`sepo-version.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/19acd881c20fb001dcd80a1a371bf3161bb41067/.agent/src/sepo-version.ts): typed validation/reader for TypeScript code.
- [`print-sepo-version.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/19acd881c20fb001dcd80a1a371bf3161bb41067/.agent/src/cli/print-sepo-version.ts): tiny workflow/diagnostic adapter.

I would keep the CLI, but keep it boring and narrow. Application code should import the reader directly; shell/workflow/bug-report paths use the CLI.

title: Add Sepo version metadata reader CLI
